oneLiner: The light, refreshing way to hit your protein target—without the gym-bro vibe.
positioningStatement: PEP is a premium, out-of-home protein drink for people who value health, efficiency, and routine. Designed as a functional nutrition tool, PEP helps disciplined, active individuals meet their protein goals with a light taste, not heaviness—supporting daily performance, hydration, and recovery with a clean, modern look.
audience: People seeking function and efficiency in nutrition—not fads

voice.tone: Clean, rational, direct, optimistic, subtly energetic

visual.photography: Minimal social cues, focus on solo or quiet active moments; product in clean, bright settings—never dark, crowded gyms or nightlife.
visual.typography: Didot (logo), Brittany Signature (tagline), Montserrat (numbers/labels), Bebas Neue (badges)
rejectLooks: Gym-bro aesthetics (black/red, muscle graphics, shaker imagery)
Lifestyle pastels or soft, playful cartoonish packaging
Party/festival visual tropes (metallics, bold vibrant cocktail cues)

inLineBrands.name: HOLY
inLineBrands.why: Functional focus on health, lightness, and vitamin-enriched profile with a simple, informative layout.

inLineBrands.name: LMNT
inLineBrands.why: Clinical, rational packaging for performance and hydration—directly communicates function and efficiency.

inLineBrands.name: NOCCO
inLineBrands.why: Supports sports and active lifestyles with bold but not 'bro-ish' branding; highlights functional benefit (BCAA, energy) over heavy aesthetics.

inLineBrands.name: VITAMIN WELL
inLineBrands.why: Clean, clear labeling with high utility and vitamin positioning—trustworthy and functional over lifestyle.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: PROTOCHOL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Blends protein with alcohol—a niche for social events, not day-to-day functional use; emphasizes social/party context over functional solo nutrition.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: BLOOM
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Prebiotic focus with playful lifestyle branding; targets wellness-treat and 'fun' occasions, not disciplined, goal-driven routines.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: EVER TREE
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Minimalist, natural soft drinks with a lifestyle relaxation angle—not functional, protein-based nutrition.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: OLIPOP
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Prebiotic sodas promoting gut health in a 'friendly' visual style; conceptually wellness/lifestyle, not a functional protein target.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: POPP
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Prebiotic 'refreshing' drink, wellness lifestyle design—leans playful and health, but not driven by function or routine.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Kin Euphorics
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Social 'euphoric' drinks supporting mood and group experience, not solo or discipline-driven use cases.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Lyre's
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Alcohol-free spirits for social and ritual occasions; all branding references social drinking, not protein-driven nutrition.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Seedlip
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Premium non-alcoholic spirits for upscale social moments—not relevant to functional, performance-optimized routines.

antiReferences.name: Lean Body (gym RTDs and similar)
antiReferences.why: Stereotypically fitness-bro protein shakes: bulk cues, aggressive marketing, 'jacked' themes—alienates broader, functional-focused audience.

antiReferences.name: drinkpep.com
antiReferences.why: Name collision; US product focused on energy/probiotic with very different positioning and legal/brand confusion risk.

antiReferences.name: Kids’ juice/energy drinks
antiReferences.why: Too playful, artificial, or sugary—undermines the rational, disciplined, aspirational core.

brandFitRules: Design must communicate functionality, health, and efficiency first.
Visuals should be clean, structured, and avoid party/lifestyle cues.
Never use gym-bro, 'jacked' or overly masculine protein tropes.
Avoid mocktail, spirit, or festival-drink looks.
Copy must emphasize solo performance, daily routine, and nutritional benefit—not social ritual or relaxation.
Never reference or imply the drink is intended for partying, nightlife, or as a treat.
No playful cartoon graphics, script overload, or pastel lifestyle visuals.
Avoid any design or copy that could be confused with kids’ drinks or energy sodas.
PEP is always pep-drink.com—never reference or draw inspiration from drinkpep.com.
